root_cause_hypothesis: Re-run after 0e1e367. The CR fix is confirmed live: <Source> now carries '' before every line break, byte-identical in shape to the platform's own flight libraries. A SECOND, same-class serialization gap remains in the AxMacroDictionary writer: it omits the empty '<Macros />' element that follows </Source>. All 644 Microsoft AxMacroDictionary files in PackagesLocalDirectory carry it; ours is the only one that does not. It compiles (build 0 errors, macro include and both #defines resolved) but will not round-trip -- Visual Studio re-adds the element on the first touch, so a golden frozen on the current output would churn. Golden therefore NOT captured; golden_pending left true, same reasoning the CR fix was made before capture. Secondary, non-blocking: validate_code(mode=references) reported '0 references verified' for a class body containing an include directive (#ConDemoModuleFlights), a macro reference (#DemoFastPostingFlight) and a global function call (isFlightEnabled) -- none of the three is resolved, so the static gate gives no signal at all on macro-based code.
suggested_fix_area: src/tools/createD365File.ts -- AxMacroDictionary writer: emit '	<Macros />' after </Source> (cover it in tests/tools/coverageClosureXml.test.ts next to the  assertion). Separately, resolveReferences: teach it the macro include/reference syntax and Global-scope function calls, or make it report 'no resolvable references' instead of a green 'all 0 verified'.
